# useToggle

A small reactive controller for explicit boolean active state.

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-3">
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Active: <strong class="text-foreground">{{ control.active.value }}</strong>
    </p>
    <div class="flex gap-2">
      <Button @click="control.activate">
        Activate
      </Button>
      <Button variant="outline" @click="control.deactivate">
        Deactivate
      </Button>
    </div>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { useToggle } from '@stacktrace/ui'
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'

const control = useToggle()
</script>
```

## Contents

- [Usage](#usage)
- [When to use](#when-to-use)
- [API Reference](#api-reference)

## Usage

Use the controller when several consumers need to read or change the same boolean state through named operations. Pass `toggle.active` to a component's controlled prop and call the methods from triggers or callbacks.

*ToggleExample.vue*

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-3">
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Active: <strong class="text-foreground">{{ control.active.value }}</strong>
    </p>
    <div class="flex gap-2">
      <Button @click="control.activate">
        Activate
      </Button>
      <Button variant="outline" @click="control.deactivate">
        Deactivate
      </Button>
    </div>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { useToggle } from '@stacktrace/ui'
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'

const control = useToggle()
</script>
```

## When to use

Prefer `useToggle` for dialogs, sheets, disclosure state, or other local active/inactive behavior. Keep request processing, validation, and domain state in their own dedicated models instead of representing them as a generic toggle.

## API Reference

### `useToggle`

Creates a controller with a readonly-by-convention active ref and explicit activation methods.

```typescript
function useToggle(initiallyActive = false): Toggle
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `initiallyActive` | `boolean` | `false` | Sets the initial value of the active ref. |

#### Returns

`Toggle` — The reactive active ref with activate and deactivate methods.

### `onActivated`

Watches a Toggle and runs a callback whenever its active ref changes to true.

```typescript
function onActivated(toggle: Toggle, callback: () => void): void
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `toggle` (required) | `Toggle` | Provides the controller to watch. |
| `callback` (required) | `() => void` | Runs when the watched controller enters the corresponding state. |

#### Returns

`void` — The helper installs a Vue watcher for the supplied controller.

### `onDeactivated`

Watches a Toggle and runs a callback whenever its active ref changes to false.

```typescript
function onDeactivated(toggle: Toggle, callback: () => void): void
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `toggle` (required) | `Toggle` | Provides the controller to watch. |
| `callback` (required) | `() => void` | Runs when the watched controller enters the corresponding state. |

#### Returns

`void` — The helper installs a Vue watcher for the supplied controller.

Activation callbacks are transition watchers: they do not run immediately for the controller's initial state.

### `Toggle`

Exposes reactive active state through explicit activation operations. Use this interface when a composed component receives an existing controller rather than creating its own state.

```typescript
interface Toggle {
  active: Ref<boolean>
  activate: () => void
  deactivate: () => void
}
```

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `active` (required) | `Ref<boolean>` | Contains the current reactive active state. |
| `activate` (required) | `() => void` | Sets active to true. |
| `deactivate` (required) | `() => void` | Sets active to false. |

Hosted documentation: [useToggle](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/composables/toggle)
